name: Migraine Disability Assessment
description: A validated questionnaire to assess headache-related disability over the past 3 months. Measures the impact of headaches on work, household activities, and social/leisure activities to help guide treatment decisions.
instructions: Please answer the following questions about ALL your headaches you have had over the last 3 months. Write your answer in the box next to each question. Write zero if you did not do the activity in the last 3 months.

questions.question: On how many days in the last 3 months did you miss work or school because of your headaches?

How many days in the last 3 months was your productivity at work or school reduced by half or more because of your headaches? (Do not include days you counted in question 1 where you missed work or school)
On how many days in the last 3 months did you not do household work because of your headaches?

How many days in the last 3 months was your productivity in household work reduced by half or more because of your headaches? (Do not include days you counted in question 3 where you did not do household work)
On how many days in the last 3 months did you miss family, social or leisure activities because of your headaches?
On how many days in the last 3 months did you have a headache? (If a headache lasted more than 1 day, count each day)
On a scale of 0–10, on average how painful were these headaches? (Where 0 = no pain at all, and 10 = pain as bad as it can be)

severityBands.label: Grade I - Minimal or Infrequent Disability
severityBands.description: Low medical need. Simple over-the-counter analgesics may be effective. Consider triptan therapy if failed on simple analgesics or have infrequent but severe migraine.

severityBands.label: Grade II - Mild or Infrequent Disability
severityBands.description: Moderate medical need. May require acute prescription medication. Consider first-line triptan medication if headaches are severe or if failed on simple analgesics.

severityBands.label: Grade III - Moderate Disability
severityBands.description: High medical need. Significant disability with severe impact on life. Specific acute therapy such as triptan is usually most appropriate. Consider prophylactic treatment.

severityBands.label: Grade IV - Severe Disability
severityBands.description: High medical need. Severe disability with major life impact. Triptan therapy recommended if suitable. Prophylactic treatment should be considered. Very high scores may indicate high frequency of non-migraine headache.

interpretation.scoringMethod: Sum questions 1-5 (total days of disability). Questions A and B provide supplementary clinical information but are not included in the MIDAS score.

supplementaryQuestions.A: Headache frequency - provides information about headache days
supplementaryQuestions.B: Average pain intensity (0-10 NRS) - provides information about headache severity

interpretation.clinicalUse: MIDAS grade predicts treatment needs and helps guide therapy selection. Take completed questionnaire to physician for treatment planning.

domains.name: Work/School
domains.description: Days missed or with reduced productivity at work/school

domains.name: Household Work
domains.description: Days missed or with reduced productivity in household work

domains.name: Family/Social/Leisure
domains.description: Days missed from family, social, or leisure activities

references.citation: Stewart WF, Lipton RB, Dowson AJ, Sawyer J. Development and testing of the Migraine Disability Assessment (MIDAS) Questionnaire to assess headache-related disability. Neurology. 2001;56(6 Suppl 1):S20-28.
Stewart WF, Lipton RB, Kolodner K, Liberman J, Sawyer J. Reliability of the migraine disability assessment score in a population-based sample of headache sufferers. Cephalalgia. 1999;19(2):107-114.
